results.title: The Mole and the lollipop
results.brief_description: Cleaning up the litter made by two candy-eating children, Mole finds a lollipop they left behind. Not knowing what it is, Mole demonstrates great inventiveness in trying to discover its purpose. An animated program without narration.
results.description: Cleaning up the litter made by two candy-eating children, Mole finds a lollipop they left behind. Not knowing what it is, Mole demonstrates great inventiveness in trying to discover its purpose. An animated program without narration.

results.title: Maps: how to read them
results.creator_credit: Sal Bruno, BFA Educational Media and Phoenix Films
results.brief_description: This film uses excellent graphics and animation to explain the concepts of map projection and latitude and longitude, to show how symbols are used on maps, and the importance of scale, legend, key, the compass role and grid references in map reading.

results.title: Abraham Lincoln. - 2nd edition.
results.creator_credit: Kathy Tallon, Scott Craig and Scott Craig Productions
results.brief_description: Beginning with the statement that Lincoln became the “last victim of the Civil War”, when he was assassinated, the film traces his life from humble beginnings in a log cabin, an axe wielding woodsman and also voracious reader as a youth, to candidate to the State Assembly at twenty-three in 1832. However, the film gives no hint of criticism of Lincoln, who spoke “with courage and conviction for all people”, and no analysis of the causes of events or action is attempted.

results.title: Fatal compromise
results.creator_credit: Josef Gemes and MAFILM Pannonia Filmstudio
results.brief_description: A superbly animated production that details the lengths to which a love-smitten hedgehog will go in order to gain the attention of a rather large feline character. However the hedgehog does not quite get what it bargained for. Suitable for primary school level.

results.title: Electricity: a first film
results.creator_credit: BFA Educational Media and Norman Bean Films
results.brief_description: Shows how electricity works, how it is generated, and the ways in which it can be utilised. Also demonstrates principles involved in making electromagnets, and safety precautions with electricity. Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: Egg-ceptional
results.brief_description: A deceptively simple film about an egg who doesn’t fit. Superb animation details the various stages of the production process where the egg who is ‘different’ fails to meet the required specifications. A film that operates on a number of levels ranging from lower primary concepts of colour, number and size to the more abstract concepts of conformity, individual differences, desire for acceptance, etc.

results.title: Desert regions: nomads and traders
results.creator_credit: Wolfgang Bayer and BFA Educational Media
results.brief_description: A comparison between two traditional cultures occupying desert lands and the ways in which both tribes customs are being forced into change. Suitable for middle and upper primary levels.

results.title: Boo Boo monster enters a beauty contest
results.creator_credit: Bill Cleary and Billy Budd Films
results.brief_description: A beautifully animated story about a friendly green monster who tries to change the colour of his skin and who learns he must accept himself as he really is. A rhythmic drum beat enhances the natural rhythm of the verse to which visuals are set. Animated by Tom Golden and Arnie Levey, music performed and composed by Herbert Harris.

results.title: Amphibians: frogs, toads and salamanders. - Rev. ed.
results.creator_credit: Paul Burnford and BFA Educational Media
results.brief_description: Uses excellent close-up photos and cinematography to show a typical amphibian life-cycle and to study the differences between three kinds of amphibians - frogs, toads and salamanders. Suitable for upper secondary level.
